heading: Grants and fellowships for food and agricultural sciences education

children.heading: Higher education teaching programs
children.label: a Higher education teaching programs
children.text: The Secretary shall promote and strengthen higher education in the food and agricultural sciences by formulating and administering programs to enhance college and university teaching programs in agriculture, natural resources, forestry, veterinary medicine, home economics, disciplines closely allied to the food and agricultural system, and rural economic, community, and business development.

to strengthen institutional capacities, including curriculum, faculty, scientific instrumentation, instruction delivery systems, and student recruitment and retention, to respond to identified State, regional, national, or international educational needs in the food and agricultural sciences, or in rural economic, community, and business development;

to attract and support undergraduate and graduate students in order to educate the students in national need areas of the food and agricultural sciences, or in rural economic, community, and business development;

to facilitate cooperative initiatives between two or more eligible institutions, or between eligible institutions and units of State government or organizations in the private sector, to maximize the development and use of resources such as faculty, facilities, and equipment to improve food and agricultural sciences teaching programs, or teaching programs emphasizing rural economic, community, and business development;

to design and implement food and agricultural programs, or programs emphasizing rural economic, community, and business development, to build teaching, research, and extension capacity at colleges and universities having significant minority enrollments;

to conduct undergraduate scholarship programs to meet national and international needs for training food and agricultural scientists and professionals, or professionals in rural economic, community, and business development; and
to conduct graduate and postdoctoral fellowship programs to attract highly promising individuals to research or teaching careers in the food and agricultural sciences.
applications for teaching enhancement projects that demonstrate enhanced coordination among all types of institutions eligible for funding under this section; and
applications for teaching enhancement projects that focus on innovative, multidisciplinary education programs, material, and curricula.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To be eligible for a grant under subsection (b), a recipient institution must have a significant demonstrable commitment to higher education teaching programs in the food and agricultural sciences, or in rural economic, community, and business development, and to each specific subject area for which the grant is to be used.

children.heading: Minority groups
children.label: 2 Minority groups
children.text: The Secretary may set aside a portion of the funds appropriated for the awarding of grants under subsection (b), and make such amounts available only for grants to eligible colleges and universities (including the University of the District of Columbia) that the Secretary determines have unique capabilities for achieving the objective of full representation of minority groups in the food and agricultural sciences workforce, or in the rural economic, community, and business development workforce, of the United States.

children.heading: Research foundations
children.label: 3 Research foundations
children.text: An eligible college or university under subsection (b) includes a research foundation maintained by the college or university.

children.heading: Food and agricultural education information system
children.label: e Food and agricultural education information system

children.text: information on enrollment, degrees awarded, faculty, and employment placement in the food and agricultural sciences; and
such other similar information as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: Evaluation of teaching programs
children.label: f Evaluation of teaching programs
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct programs to develop, analyze, and provide to colleges and universities data and information that are essential to the evaluation of the quality of teaching programs and to facilitate the design of more effective programs comprising the food and agricultural sciences higher education system of the United States.

children.heading: Continuing education
children.label: g Continuing education
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct special programs with colleges and universities, and with organizations in the private sector, to support educational initiatives to enable food and agricultural scientists and professionals to maintain their knowledge of changing technology, the expanding knowledge base, societal issues, and other factors that impact the skills and competencies needed to maintain the expertise base available to the agricultural system of the United States. The special programs shall include grants and technical assistance.

children.heading: Transfers of funds and functions
children.label: h Transfers of funds and functions
children.text: Funds authorized in (, chapter 338; ) are transferred to and shall be administered by the Secretary of Agriculture. There are transferred to the Secretary all the functions and duties of the Secretary of Education under such Act applicable to the activities and programs for which funds are made available under section 22 of such Act.section 22 of the Act of June 29, 193549 Stat. 4397 U.S.C. 329

children.heading: National Food and Agricultural Sciences Teaching, Extension, and Research Awards
children.label: i National Food and Agricultural Sciences Teaching, Extension, and Research Awards

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a National Food and Agricultural Sciences Teaching, Extension, and Research Awards program to recognize and promote excellence in teaching, extension, and research in the food and agricultural sciences at a college or university.

children.heading: Minimum requirement
children.label: B Minimum requirement
children.text: The Secretary shall make at least 1 cash award in each fiscal year to a nominee selected by the Secretary for excellence in each of the areas of teaching, extension, and research of food and agricultural science at a college or university.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 2 Funding
children.text: The Secretary may transfer funds from amounts appropriated for the conduct of any agricultural research, extension, or teaching program to an account established pursuant to this section for the purpose of making the awards. The Secretary may accept gifts in accordance with for the purpose of making the awards.section 2269 of this title

children.heading: Secondary education, 2-year postsecondary education, and agriculture in the K–12 classroom
children.label: j Secondary education, 2-year postsecondary education, and agriculture in the K–12 classroom

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: A Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given the term in .section 1001 of title 20

children.heading: Secondary school
children.label: B Secondary school
children.text: The term “secondary school” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: promote and strengthen secondary education and 2-year postsecondary education in agriscience and agribusiness in order to help ensure the existence in the United States of a qualified workforce to serve the food and agricultural sciences system; and

children.label: B
children.text: promote complementary and synergistic linkages among secondary, 2-year postsecondary, and higher education programs in the food and agricultural sciences in order to promote excellence in education and encourage more young Americans to pursue and complete a baccalaureate or higher degree in the food and agricultural sciences.

children.label: A
children.text: to enhance curricula in agricultural education;

children.label: B
children.text: to increase faculty teaching competencies;

children.label: C
children.text: to interest young people in pursuing higher education in order to prepare for scientific and professional careers in the food and agricultural sciences;

children.label: D
children.text: to promote the incorporation of agriscience and agribusiness subject matter into other instructional programs, particularly classes in science, business, and consumer education;

children.label: E
children.text: to facilitate joint initiatives by the grant recipient with other secondary schools, institutions of higher education that award an associate’s degree, and institutions of higher education that award a bachelor’s degree to maximize the development and use of resources, such as faculty, facilities, and equipment, to improve agriscience and agribusiness education;

children.label: F
children.text: to support other initiatives designed to meet local, State, regional, or national needs related to promoting excellence in agriscience and agribusiness education; and

children.label: G
children.text: to support current agriculture in the classroom programs for grades K–12.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: k Administration
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 and title XVIII of the Food and Agriculture Act of 1977 ( et seq.) shall not apply to a panel or board created for the purpose of reviewing applications and proposals for grants or nominations for awards submitted under this section.7 U.S.C. 2281

children.heading: Report
children.label: l Report
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate a biennial report detailing the distribution of funds used to implement the teaching programs under subsection (j).
$60,000,000 for each of fiscal years 1990 through 2013; and
$40,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2023.
